programmes.show_description: The artists cover Zoe's tattoo dedicated to her favourite food, Sean's lewd and lengthy leprechaun that his wife wants rid of and Chantal's pitiful pop portrait.
Lee Mack attempts an audacious hammer throw, Sarah Kendall makes sitting down memorable, and Jamali Maddix forgets his passport.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Joe Wilkinson take on Jon Richardson and Sara Pascoe. Sam Simmons joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Travis Boersma, co-founder and president of US drive-through coffee shop chain Dutch Bros, does the daily grind undercover alongside his staff.
Ex-undercover boss Eric Casaburi brings together six participants in the series who were 'busted', 'nearly busted' or 'self-busted' while working in disguise among their staff.
In Leeds, the competition features a Pavarotti-style vocal performance, home-cooked fare and fine dining, a problem preparing duck to order, and a mankini...
In Bristol, the table talk ranges from romantic meetings to naked gardening, and the purple mash is more like Play-Doh, as Colin and Mandy, PJ and Owen, and joker Geoff and Gemma compete.
...Selling: Grab your Tamagotchi's and oversized flannel tees, because Craig and Simone's home is stuck in the 90s. Can the twins drag the property back into the 21st century?
The One on a Train: Self-conscious groom Richard plans a first-class trip for his bride. Can he meet straight-talking Latrice's big hopes for a deluxe event?
The Most Romantic One Ever: Groom Ash wants to pull off the most romantic wedding ever, but his bride-to-be Vicki shuns anything corny.
Jose Costa is the president of top automotive care franchise Maaco, based in North Carolina. He dons a disguise to meet the staff who keep his firm on the road to success.
Gina Rivera is the president of Phenix Salon Suites, which rents boutiques to hairdressers. She goes incognito among her franchisees to see how the company could be improved.
Peter's: Gordon visits Peter's Italian Restaurant in New York, where the peace of Long Island is shattered by the antics of the volatile Pellegrino family who run the place.
The Mixing Bowl: Bijou New York diner The Mixing Bowl is haemorrhaging money thanks to manager Mike's out-of-control marketing plans. Can Gordon get things back on track?
...and Selling: Brand new series: Looks can be deceiving with Anthony and Rachel's home but if you dig a little deeper, the cracks start to show...
Mid-Strife Crisis: The boat is doused in glam as a drag queen duo come aboard. But will it be a show-stopping stay or will the team tank on the big stage?
Charlotte Ritchie climbs a ladder with some loo roll, Mike Wozniak tries out a new catchphrase, and Lee Mack terrifies an innocent member of the public.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Katherine Ryan take on Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson. Adam Riches as Sean Bean joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Harriet Kemsley take on guest team captain Alan Carr and Catherine Tate. Adam Buxton joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Wedding: Jeremy and Nancy (Rachel Blanchard) are getting married, for visa reasons. Mark, determined to save his friend, will do whatever it takes.
Mugging: Some muggers steal Mark's BlackBerry, which puts him off his stroke with Sophie. Meanwhile, Jeremy faces a tricky dilemma over the love of his life and a threesome.
Hayley's cheeky cartoon tattoo is threatening to derail her wedding plans and Mick wants to cover an X-rated inking that's causing embarrassment to his daughter.
Charlotte Ritchie climbs a ladder with some loo roll, Mike Wozniak tries out a new catchphrase, and Lee Mack terrifies an innocent member of the public.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Katherine Ryan take on Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson. Adam Riches as Sean Bean joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Shane Evans, the co-founder and chief operating officer of Massage Heights, one of the biggest massage therapy chains in North America, goes undercover in her own company.
Bruce 'Two Dogs' Bozsum, chairman of the board of a Native American gaming and entertainment empire, goes undercover to ensure that the company's casinos are thriving.
In Rugby, party queens Soroya and Jodie's unusual pie horrifies super-confident Raj. And estate agent Michael and Caroline search for flaws in Raj and Freddie's 'perfect' relationship.
In South Yorkshire, Phil and Ali host an evening fit for the A-list, there's romance in the air for Erica and Nigel, and art lovers Stephen and Kevin sculpt a sophisticated trifle.
...Selling: City living is draining the bank account for Joe and Lana, even with Joe working seven days a week. Can the bros turn their property from rags to riches?
The Wet 'n' Wild Weymouth One: Leon wants to get wed on a beach in Weymouth. But bride-to-be Kirsty wants a fairy-tale day somewhere special to her.
The Halloween Special One: Sam wants a witchy wedding in the woods. But traditional bride Ezra has her heart set on a fairy-tale event.
David Seelinger, the chairman and CEO of New Jersey-based EmpireCLS, which provides chauffeured luxury transportation, works among his staff posing as a reality show contestant.
Robert Powells, the president of Rocket Fizz Soda Pop and Candy Shops, the largest fizzy drink and sweet shop franchise in America, goes incognito among his staff.
Seascape: Gordon Ramsay visits the Seascape restaurant in the seaside village of Islip, on Long Island in New York, where owner Peter has nearly a million dollars of debt.
The Secret Garden: Gordon Ramsay visits a romantic French restaurant in California, where temperamental chef Michel Bardavid's complicated cuisine is keeping the customers away.
...and Selling: Brand new series: Kim and Pete find it hard to raise their boys in a split-level property. As they try to level up, can the bros help their home reach new heights?
Rules of En-rage-ment: The deck team band together to pull off a dazzling drag show for their guests. But a night out lands some crew members in hot water.
Greg Davies and Alex Horne set their captive band of comics humiliating missions involving cling film, fashion for insects, and face-based shape-shifting.
...Christmas Special: Jimmy Carr hosts a festive edition, with Susie Dent and Rachel Riley. Jon Richardson and Sarah Millican take on Sean Lock and David Mitchell. Plus: David O'Doherty.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Katherine Ryan and Joe Lycett take on Sean Lock and Johnny Vegas. Christopher Bliss joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Sectioning: Jeremy is forced to consider getting an old friend sectioned. And he soon realises sectioning could be a good way of getting rid of other problem people...
Shrooming: Jeremy plans to throw a secret magic mushroom party in the flat, where he hopes to seduce his ex, Big Suze.
